# LLM


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

## LLM rewrite

Ask an LLM to rewrite text according to a goal description. Uses the
OpenRouter API so any supported model can be swapped in.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/hugobellamy/text_diff/blob/main/text_diff/llm.py#L23"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### get_llm_rewrite

``` python

async def get_llm_rewrite(
    problem_description:str, current_text:str, api_key:str, model:str=''
)->str:

```

*Ask an LLM to rewrite `current_text` to achieve `problem_description`.*

Returns the rewritten text string.

## Semantic grouping via LLM

After computing the diff, we can optionally ask an LLM to group
semantically related changes together. For example, if changing a
subject also requires changing the verb, those two replacements should
be presented as one logical edit.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/hugobellamy/text_diff/blob/main/text_diff/llm.py#L66"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### get_semantic_groups

``` python

async def get_semantic_groups(
    diff_items:List, api_key:str, model:str=''
)->List:

```

*Ask the LLM which diff items should be grouped together.*

Returns a list of groups, where each group is a list of indices to
merge.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/hugobellamy/text_diff/blob/main/text_diff/llm.py#L126"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### llm_semantic_grouping

``` python

async def llm_semantic_grouping(
    ops:List, api_key:str, model:str=''
)->List:

```

*Use an LLM to intelligently group semantically related diff ops.*

Wraps
[`get_semantic_groups`](https://hugobellamy.github.io/text_diff/llm.html#get_semantic_groups) +
[`apply_semantic_groups`](https://hugobellamy.github.io/text_diff/group.html#apply_semantic_groups).
Falls back to returning `ops` unchanged if the LLM call fails.

## Diff pipeline

[`process_diff`](https://hugobellamy.github.io/text_diff/llm.html#process_diff)
takes a raw original and rewritten text and runs the full cleanup →
grouping → newline extraction → flagging pipeline.

[`request_text_improvement`](https://hugobellamy.github.io/text_diff/llm.html#request_text_improvement)
is the top-level function: it asks the LLM to rewrite, then diffs and
processes the result.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/hugobellamy/text_diff/blob/main/text_diff/llm.py#L162"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### process_diff

``` python

async def process_diff(
    original:str, rewritten:str, api_key:str='', model:str='', semantic_grouping:bool=True
)->List:

```

*Run the full diff pipeline: diff -\> clean -\> group -\> extract
newlines -\> flag -\> (optional) LLM grouping.*

Set `semantic_grouping=False` to skip the LLM semantic grouping step.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/hugobellamy/text_diff/blob/main/text_diff/llm.py#L200"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### request_text_improvement

``` python

async def request_text_improvement(
    problem_description:str, current_text:str, api_key:str='', model:str='', semantic_grouping:bool=True
)->List:

```

*End-to-end: LLM rewrite then diff pipeline.*

1.  Ask LLM to rewrite `current_text` according to `problem_description`
2.  Compute and process the diff between original and rewritten

Returns a list of diff ops ready for display.

## Tests

The LLM functions require an API key so we can’t run them in CI. We test
[`process_diff`](https://hugobellamy.github.io/text_diff/llm.html#process_diff)
without semantic grouping to validate the pipeline, and test
[`request_text_improvement`](https://hugobellamy.github.io/text_diff/llm.html#request_text_improvement)
error handling.

``` python
# process_diff without LLM grouping
result = await process_diff("the old cat", "the new cat", semantic_grouping=False)

# Should have equal + replace + equal, all with accepted flags
types = [op["type"] for op in result]
assert "replace" in types
assert all("accepted" in op for op in result)

# equal parts should be accepted, replace should not
for op in result:
    if op["type"] == "equal":
        assert op["accepted"] == True
    elif op["type"] == "replace":
        assert op["accepted"] == False
```

``` python
# identical text -> all equal
result = await process_diff("same text", "same text", semantic_grouping=False)
assert all(op["type"] == "equal" for op in result)
assert all(op["accepted"] == True for op in result)
```

``` python
# missing API key returns error ops (temporarily clear env var)
_saved_key = os.environ.pop("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", None)
result = await request_text_improvement("fix grammar", "hello world", api_key="")
if _saved_key: os.environ["OPENROUTER_API_KEY"] = _saved_key
assert any("API Key missing" in op.get("text", "") for op in result)
```

``` python
# process_diff with multiline text
result = await process_diff("line one\nline two", "line one\nline three", semantic_grouping=False)
# Should contain a newline equal block
assert any(op.get("text") == "\n" and op["type"] == "equal" for op in result)
```
